items.title: Basrah crude prices climb with global oil rally
items.desc: Shafaq News- Basrah Iraq’s Basrah crude prices rose more than 4% on Wednesday, tracking gains in global oil markets after the United States and Iran exchanged strikes, according to oil price data reviewed by Shafaq News. items.title: US and Iran trade blows in eleventh night
items.desc: Shafaq News- Washington/ Tehran The United States and Iran exchanged attacks for an 11th consecutive night, with Washington confirming operations against Iranian military and naval infrastructure before Tehran launched a retaliatory drone strike against a US military base in Kuwait. items.title: Oil prices climb to five-week high on US-Iran conflict
items.desc: Shafaq News Oil prices extended gains on Wednesday as fears of further supply disruptions intensified after U.S. forces struck Iranian military targets for the 11th straight night, while oil tankers made U-turns in the Red Sea after warnings by Iran-backed Houthi militia. items.title: Damascus Jewish quarter returns to the spotlight
items.desc: Shafaq News- Damascus In the eastern part of Damascus' Old City, just a short walk from Bab Sharqi (the Eastern Gate), the Jewish Quarter remains defined by its narrow stone alleys and traditional Damascene houses with inner courtyards, preserving the neighborhood's architectural character despite the departure of most of its longtime residents. items.title: Baghdad dismantles two child trafficking networks
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq's Interior Ministry said on Tuesday that its counter-trafficking directorate had dismantled two criminal networks involved in trafficking and unlawfully exploiting children in Baghdad and other provinces. items.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires his military chief after protests and names a new one
